title: Experience Asset
description: The general, tool-agnostic atom of Org_system. One captured, verifiable unit of work experience — grabbed automatically from an AI tool a user works through, stored in SYNAPSE memory, and served as context to a colleague's AI tool. Domain-specific fields (e.g. simulation) live under `domain_extension` so the core stays general across every tool and workflow.

source.description: Which AI tool / connector this experience was captured from.

connector.description: The ToolConnector adapter id that captured it, e.g. 'mcp-gateway', 'idynomics2-adapter'.

actor.description: WHOSE experience this is. Drives attribution ('whose experiences am I using') and who-knows-what.

task.description: What the user was trying to do.

goal.description: Plain-language objective, e.g. 'make Postia colony expand spatially'.

trace_summary.description: LLM-distilled narrative of what actually happened (actions, key decisions, dead ends). Produced automatically at capture — this is the 'grab information automatically' output.

outcome.description: Did it work? The raw signal the verifier uses.

signal.description: How we know, e.g. 'tests passed', 'run reproduced', 'user marked good', 'ticket closed'.

content.description: The reusable payload: the actual experience a colleague's AI should inherit.

what_failed.description: Negative result — the most valuable, least-recorded asset.

rationale.description: The 'why' (Polanyi target). Explains; never certifies. Trust comes from `verification`.

domain_extension.description: Domain-specific structured fields. For simulation, this is the Simulation Experience Asset (see schemas/simulation_experience_asset.schema.json). Kept separate so the core Experience Asset is general across all tools/workflows.

$ref_schema.description: URI of the domain schema these fields conform to.

verification.description: How this experience is checked. Simple now; pluggable per domain.

method.description: Phase 0 default per domain: simulation -> rerun_and_compare, general -> llm_judge or outcome_signal.

reverify_after_days.description: Rot control: re-check on a schedule.

visibility.description: Permissions — WHOSE experience is served to WHOM. Answers the surveillance/consent tension.

memory.description: Linkage into SYNAPSE. Set by the storage layer, not the capture layer.

usage.description: Consumption stats that power the dashboards.

served_to.description: Actor ids who consumed this experience (for 'whose experiences am I using').
